mainmatter.p: Nathan Corbyn arrived in Cambridge; met with him in the afternoon.

frontmatter.title: 2023-10-05 / higher duploid theory: “shut up and calculate”

mainmatter.p: Meeting with Fosco Loregian and Daniele Palombi. Fosco Loregian has suggested that we should tackle our question by thinking of duploids as adjunctions; then we are trying to find an adjunction-theoretic universal property for the “equalizing” completion \tilde{F} \dashv \tilde{G} of an adjunction F \dashv G.

Let F \dashv G \colon D \to C be an adjunction, and let T : \equiv GF \colon C \to C and S : \equiv FG \colon D \to D be the corresponding monad and comonad respectively. Then we have a new adjunction G' \dashv F' \colon C_T \to D_S respectively.

Let H be the collage of the distributor \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } \colon C ^{ \mathsf{o} } \times D \to \mathbf{Set} of oblique maps specified as \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } { \left ( P,N \right )} : \equiv C { \left ( P,GN \right )}, so that we have:
mainmatter.tex: \begin{aligned} H { \left ( P,Q \right )} &= C { \left ( P,Q \right )} \\ H { \left ( M,N \right )} &= D { \left ( M,N \right )} \\ H { \left ( P,N \right )} &= \mathcal{O} _{ F \dashv G } { \left ( P,N \right )} = C { \left ( P,GN \right )} \\ H { \left ( N,P \right )} &= \varnothing \end{aligned}
mainmatter.p: Let K be the collage of the distributor \mathcal{O} _{ G' \dashv F' } \colon D_S ^{ \mathsf{o} } \times C_T \to \mathbf{Set} specified as \mathcal{O} _{ G' \dashv F' } { \left ( N,P \right )} : \equiv D_S { \left ( N,F'P \right )}, so that we have:
The initial conjecture of Fosco Loregian is that we can form some kind of square like the following, in which the diagonal map is the unit of the equalizing completion of an adjunction:
It is unclear if the square above is supposed to commute fully or only laxly. But if something happens here, maybe there is a universal property.

Had a very interesting chat with Nathan Corbyn and Marcelo Fiore this afternoon. Nathan Corbyn described his work on normalization-by-evaluation in gluing style, which he has formalized computationally in Agda via setoids. Nathan Corbyn aims to capture TDPE in a principled way, including the Futamura projections(!); Marcelo Fiore is suggesting that we start from the free extension of \lambda-calculus by a model of a given algebraic theory (which I think we basically understand), and try to move from this to handling second-order algebraic theories. We will come back to this tomorrow.
Received an email from Marco Paviotti suggesting a scientific meeting on guarded domain theory in 2024, to be held in Kent.
I had an idea for a potential student project about contractibility; started a page to collect such project ideas.
I remembered to write down a very tasty sauce recipe I invented the other night.

I responded to Yue Niu’s email regarding his research statement. I also responded to Lingyuan Ye, suggesting a meeting next Friday for him to explain his work on the categorical structure in the theory of arithmetic to me.

Received some helpful additional information from Thorsten Altenkirch about the work Stefania Damato presented at Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar #32. Corrected a few mistakes in my notes from that talk.

frontmatter.title: Stefania Damato on a container model of type theory

meta.name: venue

link.title: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar #32

meta.link: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar #32

mainmatter.p: Stefania Damato describes a model of type theory in (generalized) containers, joint work with her PhD supervisor Thorsten Altenkirch. The background of this work is the prior functorial semantics of QIITs due to Thorsten Altenkirch, Paolo Capriotti, Gabe Dijkstra, Nicolai Kraus, and Fredrik Nordvall Foresberg.

In that prior work, the universal property of QIITs is described in terms of certain functors having initial algebras, but the question of whether initial algebras in fact exist in usual semantic categories was not satisfactorily dealt with. In the context of ordinary inductive types, presentation by a container is a sufficient notion (i.e. the functor should be polynomial). In this work, Stefania Damato and Thorsten Altenkirch have needed to generalize the notion of polynomial in a way that Nicola Gambino suggested is equivalent to familial representability.

The motivation for finding a model of type theory in containers is that this is apparently necessary in order to be able to use type theoretic constructs in the signature of a QIIT. I never did fully understand how this relates to the constructs that appear in the constructors of the QIIT once its initial algebra is computed, as these happen in different models of type theory: the QIIT is specified, apparently, in the container model but then the QIIT is used in (e.g.) the \mathbf{Set} model. I chatted with Stefania Damato afterward and she offered some helpful answers, but I don’t think I fully understood this point.
